title: acalypha scleropumila
intro: Acalypha scleropumila, also known as the 'Spiny Copperleaf', is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its spiny leaves and reddish-purple flowers. Traditionally, Acalypha scleropumila has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and providing relief from respiratory issues. Acalypha scleropumila can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and has also been used in traditional medicine as a poultice or topical application for skin conditions.
